title: Compact Creamy Irish Potato Casserole
description: A pungent and dense Irish dish featuring light brown soft sugar and salt-bake potatoes, perfect for a show-off dinner party. The combination of creamy sauce and crispy top makes it a delightful treat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, combine the sliced potatoes and enough cold water to cover them, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the potatoes are slightly tender.
Drain the potatoes and set them aside to cool.
In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to combine, cooking for 1 minute.
Slowly pour in the milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it thickens, stirring occasionally.
Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in the grated cheese until melted and smooth.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potatoes, cheese sauce, light brown soft sugar, salt, and pepper, and mix until the potatoes are well coated.
Transfer the potato mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and top with additional grated cheese if desired.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is heated through.
